description: In August 2023, Zoom Video Communications, a company built on facilitating remote work, mandated that employees living within 50 miles of an office must return at least two days a week. This move triggered a significant backlash among the tech workforce and public discourse regarding the hypocrisy of 'remote-first' corporate messaging vs. reality.

key_events.description: Zoom issues official August 2023 memo requiring hybrid attendance for local staff.
Global media outlets highlight the irony of the 'Zoom' company forcing office returns.
Internal employee town halls leak to the press showing widespread staff frustration.
Competitors in the tech sector publicly contrast their 'remote-first' policies against Zoom's mandate.

calibration_notes: This scenario is useful for calibrating models on corporate credibility and the limits of executive authority when the company's product directly enables the very behavior it is banning.
